description: Consolidate all copies of pluginNameFromPackage to import from @opentabs-dev/shared. PR #43 moved the function to shared/constants.ts but left the CLI's duplicate copy and a redundant re-export chain through loader.ts. After this work, pluginNameFromPackage has exactly one definition (in shared) and all consumers import it directly from @opentabs-dev/shared.

userStories.title: Replace CLI's local pluginNameFromPackage with shared import
userStories.description: Delete the duplicated pluginNameFromPackage function from the CLI and import it from @opentabs-dev/shared instead. Update the module export to re-export from shared. Update the test file to import from @opentabs-dev/shared for the pluginNameFromPackage tests.
acceptanceCriteria: No local definition of pluginNameFromPackage exists in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts
pluginNameFromPackage is imported from @opentabs-dev/shared in platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts
pluginNameFromPackage is re-exported from the module's export block (so downstream consumers like plugin.test.ts still work via ./plugin.js)
PLUGIN_PREFIX is removed from the @opentabs-dev/shared import if no other code in plugin.ts uses it
All 4 pluginNameFromPackage tests in plugin.test.ts still pass
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts (delete local function, update imports and exports)
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.test.ts (update import source for pluginNameFromPackage)

Context:
PR #43 moved pluginNameFromPackage to @opentabs-dev/shared (platform/shared/src/constants.ts) and updated the MCP server and plugin-tools to import from there. But the CLI has its own identical copy that was not updated.

Changes:

1. In platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts:
   - Add `pluginNameFromPackage` to the existing @opentabs-dev/shared import block (lines 9-20):
     ```
     import {
       type ConfigSchema,
       type ConfigSettingDefinition,
       DEFAULT_HOST,
       isWindows,
       normalizePluginName,
       PLATFORM_PACKAGES,
       PLUGIN_PREFIX,
       pluginNameFromPackage,
       resolvePluginPackageCandidates,
       TOOLS_FILENAME,
       toErrorMessage,
     } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
     ```
   - Delete the local function definition (lines 693-709 — the JSDoc comment starting with `/** Derive the short plugin name...` through the closing `};`)
   - Check if PLUGIN_PREFIX is still used elsewhere in the file after deleting the function. If not, remove it from the import. (Grep for PLUGIN_PREFIX in the file — it may be used in other functions like resolvePackageName.)
   - Keep pluginNameFromPackage in the export block at line 1157 — it's now a re-export of the shared import, which is fine. The test file and potentially other consumers import it from ./plugin.js.

2. In platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.test.ts:
   - Change the import of pluginNameFromPackage from `./plugin.js` to `@opentabs-dev/shared`. Current code (line 13):
     ```
     import {
       buildDirectLookupCandidates,
       findPluginDir,
       pluginNameFromPackage,
       readLocalPluginInfo,
       ...
     } from './plugin.js';
     ```
   - Move `pluginNameFromPackage` out of the ./plugin.js import and into a new or existing @opentabs-dev/shared import. The file already has an @opentabs-dev/shared import at line 7:
     ```
     import { normalizePluginName, resolvePluginPackageCandidates } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
     ```
   - Add pluginNameFromPackage to that import.

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove pluginNameFromPackage from the export block in plugin.ts — other modules may import it from there.
- Do NOT modify the test assertions — only the import source changes.
- The comment `/** Derive the short plugin name from a package name. Mirrors pluginNameFromPackage in mcp-server/src/loader.ts. */` must be deleted along with the function body.

userStories.title: Remove pluginNameFromPackage re-export from loader.ts and update consumers
userStories.description: Remove the redundant re-export of pluginNameFromPackage from loader.ts. Update plugin-management.ts and loader.test.ts to import directly from @opentabs-dev/shared instead of going through the loader re-export.
acceptanceCriteria: pluginNameFromPackage is not in the export block of platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts (remove from export block)
- platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts (change import source)
- platform/mcp-server/src/loader.test.ts (change import source)

Context:
After PR #43, loader.ts imports pluginNameFromPackage from @opentabs-dev/shared and re-exports it. This re-export exists only for backward compatibility with plugin-management.ts and loader.test.ts. Since those files should import from shared directly (like discovery.test.ts already does), the re-export is unnecessary indirection.

Changes:

1. In platform/mcp-server/src/loader.ts, remove `pluginNameFromPackage` from the export block. Current code (lines 425-434):
   ```
   export {
     checkBrowserToolReferences,
     checkSdkCompatibility,
     extractToolsArray,
     loadPlugin,
     parseMajorMinor,
     pluginNameFromPackage,
     validateTools,
   };
   ```
   Remove the `pluginNameFromPackage,` line. Also remove `pluginNameFromPackage` from the import block at the top (lines 15-26) since loader.ts still uses it internally at line 270 (`const pluginName = pluginNameFromPackage(pkg.name);`). Wait — actually check: does loader.ts USE pluginNameFromPackage internally? If yes, keep the import but remove the re-export. Read the file to find all usages.

   Actually, loader.ts calls pluginNameFromPackage at line ~270:
   ```
   const pluginName = pluginNameFromPackage(pkg.name);
   ```
   So KEEP the import from @opentabs-dev/shared, just remove it from the export block.

2. In platform/mcp-server/src/plugin-management.ts, change the import source. Current code (line 23):
   ```
   import { pluginNameFromPackage } from './loader.js';
   ```
   Replace with importing from @opentabs-dev/shared. The file already imports from @opentabs-dev/shared (lines 13-22):
   ```
   import {
     atomicWrite,
     getConfigDir,
     getConfigPath,
     isValidPluginPackageName,
     isWindows,
     normalizePluginName,
     PLATFORM_PACKAGES,
     resolvePluginPackageCandidates,
   } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
   ```
   Add `pluginNameFromPackage` to this import block. Delete the `import { pluginNameFromPackage } from './loader.js';` line entirely.

3. In platform/mcp-server/src/loader.test.ts, change the import source. Current code (line 5):
   ```
   import { checkSdkCompatibility, loadPlugin, parseMajorMinor, pluginNameFromPackage, validateTools } from './loader.js';
   ```
   Move `pluginNameFromPackage` out of the ./loader.js import and into a new @opentabs-dev/shared import:
   ```
   import { pluginNameFromPackage } from '@opentabs-dev/shared';
   import { checkSdkCompatibility, loadPlugin, parseMajorMinor, validateTools } from './loader.js';
   ```

Constraints:
- Do NOT remove the pluginNameFromPackage import in loader.ts — it's still used internally (line ~270). Only remove the re-export.
- Do NOT modify any test assertions or function logic — only import sources change.

userStories.title: Remove duplicate pluginNameFromPackage tests from loader.test.ts
userStories.description: Remove the pluginNameFromPackage describe block from loader.test.ts since discovery.test.ts already has a comprehensive superset of the same tests (9 tests vs 4, all importing from @opentabs-dev/shared).
acceptanceCriteria: No describe('pluginNameFromPackage') block exists in platform/mcp-server/src/loader.test.ts
The pluginNameFromPackage describe block in platform/mcp-server/src/discovery.test.ts is unchanged (9 tests)
All remaining tests in loader.test.ts still pass
